name: Can I sue my boss if I have breathing problems from fumes at work?

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my boss if I have breathing problems from fumes at work?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, workers' comp is typically exclusive; sue only for intentional acts or third parties like faulty equipment providers.
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Workers' compensation is usually your exclusive remedy against your employer, barring lawsuits unless intentional harm is proven.**

In most states including Utah and Idaho, workers' comp covers respiratory issues from workplace exposure without fault proof, trading no-sue rights for benefits[1][2]. Suing requires rare 'intentional tort' evidence like deliberate exposure[1]. Third-party suits may apply if equipment makers or contractors are negligent[2].

- Benefits include medical costs, lost wages, disability.
- No pain/suffering damages in standard comp claims.
- OSHA violations can support claims but don't enable suits.

File your comp claim promptly; a lawyer can check for exceptions or third-party options. Most cases stay in workers' comp[1][2].

description: No, workers' comp is typically exclusive; sue only for intentional acts or third parties like faulty equipment providers.
